fendi.heritage: Founded in Rome in 1925 by Adele and Edoardo Fendi. Known for exceptional fur craftsmanship and Italian leather artistry.
fendi.creative_direction: Kim Jones (RTW and Men's), Silvia Venturini Fendi (Accessories and Fur). Karl Lagerfeld served as creative director for 54 years until 2019.
fendi.signature_codes: FF monogram, Selleria hand-stitching, Peekaboo bag, Baguette bag, intarsia fur
fendi.key_products: Baguette bag (1997, revived 2019), Peekaboo bag, Selleria leather goods, FF monogram RTW and accessories, First bag
fendi.brand_positioning: Roman luxury house combining artisanal craft heritage with contemporary fashion energy. Operates at the intersection of craft legacy and cultural relevance.
fendi.china_presence: Strong in Shanghai, Beijing, Chengdu; the Baguette and FF monogram have significant XHS presence among 22-38 age group.

dior.heritage: Founded in Paris in 1947 by Christian Dior. The New Look silhouette defined post-war fashion. House of couture with strong RTW and accessories presence.
dior.creative_direction: Maria Grazia Chiuri (Women's RTW and Couture). Kim Jones (Men's).
dior.signature_codes: Bar jacket, Toile de Jouy, Cannage quilting, CD logo, Saddle bag, Lady Dior bag
dior.key_products: Lady Dior bag, Saddle bag, Book Tote, 30 Montaigne bag, Bar jacket, Oblique canvas accessories
dior.brand_positioning: French couture house balancing archive femininity with contemporary relevance. Strong in accessories and the CD monogram.
dior.china_presence: One of the top-performing luxury brands in China; strong across all tier-1 cities.

chanel.heritage: Founded by Gabrielle 'Coco' Chanel in Paris. Defined modern luxury with the little black dress, No.5 perfume, and tweed suits.
chanel.creative_direction: Do not reference current creative director — uncertain at time of this brief's generation.
chanel.signature_codes: Double-C logo, tweed suiting, quilted lambskin, chain-strap bags, camellia flower
chanel.key_products: Classic Flap bag, 2.55 bag, Boy Chanel bag, tweed jacket, No.5 perfume, Coco Crush jewellery
chanel.brand_positioning: The apex French luxury house. Resists discounting and trend cycles. Clients buy into a value system, not a seasonal story.
chanel.china_presence: Aspirational and aspirationally exclusive. Waitlists and price increases have become part of brand mythology in China.

louis_vuitton.heritage: Founded in Paris in 1854 as a luggage maker. The LV monogram (1896) is one of the most recognisable luxury codes in the world.
louis_vuitton.creative_direction: Nicolas Ghesquière (Women's RTW). Pharrell Williams (Men's RTW).
louis_vuitton.signature_codes: LV monogram, Damier check, Epi leather, Vuitton trunks, LVMH group flagship
louis_vuitton.key_products: Neverfull tote, Speedy bag, Capucines bag, Twist bag, Dauphine bag, LV Trainer sneaker, Archlight sneaker
louis_vuitton.brand_positioning: The world's highest-revenue luxury brand. Breadth of product from entry-level accessories to couture-adjacent RTW.
louis_vuitton.china_presence: Dominant across all Chinese luxury segments; strong gifting culture around LV accessories.

bottega_veneta.heritage: Founded in 1966 in Vicenza, Italy. 'When your own initials are enough' — logo-free philosophy built on intricate craft.
bottega_veneta.signature_codes: Intrecciato woven leather, no visible logo, Parachute bag, Andiamo bag, Jodie bag, woven textures
bottega_veneta.key_products: Jodie bag, Andiamo bag, The Pouch, Intrecciato leather goods, woven sandals and mules
bottega_veneta.brand_positioning: Quiet luxury benchmark. Appeals to clients who have 'moved past' logos. Craft and material quality are the entire conversation.
bottega_veneta.china_presence: Growing rapidly among second-generation luxury consumers who want to signal sophistication without visible branding.

celine.heritage: Founded in Paris in 1945. Repositioned as a minimalist luxury powerhouse under Phoebe Philo, then redefined again under Hedi Slimane.
celine.creative_direction: Hedi Slimane (Creative Director, Artistic, Creative and Image Director).
celine.signature_codes: Arc de Triomphe logo (current), Saint Laurent-influenced rock and preppy aesthetic, Triomphe canvas
celine.key_products: Triomphe bag, Classique 16 bag, Tabou bag, Triomphe canvas accessories, knit and tailoring RTW
celine.brand_positioning: French house with a strong visual identity and devoted following. Current direction is rock-inflected Parisian chic.
celine.china_presence: Strong among fashion-forward clients 25-40 who follow European runway closely.

loewe.heritage: Founded in Madrid in 1846. One of the oldest luxury leather goods houses in Europe. Joined LVMH in 1996.
loewe.signature_codes: Puzzle bag, Hammock bag, Anagram logo, Spanish leather craft, art-adjacent campaigns and packaging
loewe.key_products: Puzzle bag, Hammock bag, Basket bag, Flamenco bag, Gate bag, Anagram leather goods
loewe.brand_positioning: Intellectual luxury. Attracts culturally curious clients who value craft alongside conceptual design. Jonathan Anderson's direction is design-forward and art-adjacent.
loewe.china_presence: Fastest-growing luxury brand on XHS among 22-35 creatives and fashion insiders. Puzzle bag has strong cult status.

stone_island.heritage: Founded in 1982 in Ravarino, Italy by Massimo Osti. Built around garment dyeing, technical fabrics, and functional outerwear.
stone_island.creative_direction: Carlo Rivetti (President). No single named creative director — design is collaborative and research-driven.
stone_island.signature_codes: Compass badge (removable arm badge), garment dyeing, Ghost piece dyeing, TC fabric, Nylon Metal
stone_island.key_products: Garment-dyed outerwear, Nylon Metal jacket, Ghost pieces, Ice Shell jacket, technical knitwear
stone_island.brand_positioning: Research-led technical outerwear. The badge is worn by those who understand what it represents. Sits at the intersection of sportswear culture and high craft.
stone_island.china_presence: Growing among male luxury consumers 22-38 in tier-1 cities, especially those with UK/European fashion awareness.

tiffany.heritage: Founded in New York in 1837. Defined American luxury jewellery. The blue box is one of the most recognised luxury symbols globally.
tiffany.creative_direction: Acquired by LVMH in 2021. Alexandre Arnault (EVP Product and Communications). Do not reference a specific Creative Director.
tiffany.signature_codes: Tiffany Blue (PMS 1837), blue box, yellow diamond, Elsa Peretti designs, Jean Schlumberger archive
tiffany.key_products: Tiffany T collection, HardWear collection, Return to Tiffany, Elsa Peretti Bone cuff, engagement rings, yellow diamond jewellery
tiffany.brand_positioning: American luxury jewellery repositioned under LVMH for a younger, fashion-aware audience while protecting heritage codes.
tiffany.china_presence: Strong gifting occasion brand. Growing in fine jewellery among clients 25-40 who want American luxury with cultural cachet.

amiri.heritage: Founded in Los Angeles in 2014 by Mike Amiri. Rock and roll DNA, hand-distressed denim, American luxury with West Coast attitude.
amiri.signature_codes: MX1 jeans, paint-splatter distressing, Sunset logo, bandana print, camo, eagle motif
amiri.key_products: MX1 jeans, track jackets, MA-1 bomber, Skel sneaker, Arts District sneaker, bandana-print accessories
amiri.brand_positioning: American luxury streetwear at couture price points. Rock sensibility, LA cool, hand-craft details. Appeals to clients who want edge alongside luxury.
amiri.china_presence: Growing in tier-1 cities among male clients 22-38 who follow American streetwear and music culture.
